rows.text: % difference from comp CAD market value
Median adjusted value ($/SF — based on Subject SF)
Economies of scale: bigger homes have lower $/sqft (fixed costs — kitchen, roof, mechanicals — amortize over more square footage). Larger comp adjusts up toward
Newer comp adjusts down; older comp adjusts up. Uses effective year built when the CAD publishes it — a renovated 1967 home with an effective year of 2007 compa
Adjusts for CAD-published land valuation differences between the subject and comp. The cap prevents a single high-land-value comp from collapsing the baseline o
Pool, garage spaces, fireplace, and similar features are adjusted in dollar terms when the CAD publishes the flag. Values match HCAD/DCAD published methodology.
No single comp can adjust more than 30% from its unadjusted $/sqft. Keeps the brief defensible against a CAD reviewer who would challenge a 50% swing on any one
